samples.xml_description:
Implement a module for parallel-to-serial conversion, where every four input bits are converted to a serial one bit output (from MSB to LSB). The output signal valid_out is set to 1 to indicate the availability of valid serial output. When valid_out = 1, the most significant bit of d is output, and the remaining three bits are output sequentially in the following 3 cycles. Clock signal used for synchronous operations. Reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive. 4-bit parallel data input. Valid signal indicating the availability of serial output. Serial output representing the converted data. Width of parallel data input. Count of cycles for conversion.

Initialize the counter (cnt) to 0 and valid signal (valid) to 0.

When the reset signal (rst_n) is 1, increment the counter (cnt) by 1.

When the counter (cnt) is 3, indicating the last bit of the parallel input, update the data register (data) with the parallel input (d), reset the counter (cnt) to 0, and set the valid signal (valid) to 1.

Otherwise, increment the counter (cnt) by 1, set the valid signal (valid) to 0, and shift the data register (data) one bit to the left, with the most significant bit shifted to the least significant bit.
